17699359.summary: On 21 July 2026, Greencoat Renewables repurchased 271,109 ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin at prices ranging from €0.7520 to €0.7720 per share, with a volume-weighted average price of €0.7634. The shares will be cancelled. Following settlement, the company holds 200,000 treasury shares and has 1,082,834,285 shares in issue. The transaction forms part of the buyback programme announced 5 March 2026.
17699359.brief: Greencoat Renewables purchased 271,109 shares for cancellation as part of its March 2026 buyback programme.
17699359.take: Routine capital management activity; share buybacks typically signal management confidence but are non-events absent specific context around valuation or strategic initiatives.

17699357.summary: Venture Life Group PLC purchased 190,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at 70 pence per share via Cavendish Capital Markets, to be held as treasury shares. Following the repurchase, the company has 128.86 million shares in issue, with 7.95 million held in treasury, reducing voting rights to 120.91 million shares. The buyback was conducted under the share repurchase programme announced 30 September 2025.
17699357.brief: Venture Life repurchases 190,000 shares at 70p per share under existing buyback programme.
17699357.take: Routine capital management action; modest buyback at consistent price signals orderly execution but provides limited insight into management confidence or strategic direction for this healthcare products company.

17699356.summary: AEP Plantations purchased 45,977 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at prices ranging from 164p to 174p, with a volume-weighted average price of 167.32p, through Cavendish Capital Markets. Since the buyback programme's announcement on 6 July, the company has acquired 580,292 shares held in treasury. Following this purchase, 383,013,188 voting shares remain in issue, with 16,749,532 shares held in treasury.
17699356.brief: AEP Plantations bought 45,977 shares at 164-174p on 21 July under its buyback programme.
17699356.take: Routine share buyback execution within announced programme parameters; ongoing capital return activity with no material news flow or strategic implications for shareholders.

17699353.summary: Science Group plc purchased 10,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at 585 pence per share through Panmure Liberum Limited, representing 0.02% of voting rights. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the purchase, 40,662,593 shares remain in issue (excluding 5,523,281 treasury shares), with the company now holding approximately 12% of issued shares in treasury.
17699353.brief: Science Group PLC repurchased 10,000 shares at 585p per share on 21 July 2026.
17699353.take: Routine share buyback continuation; modest capital allocation signaling management confidence in valuation, though immaterial scale suggests incremental rather than transformational capital deployment.

17699352.summary: Pantheon International PLC purchased 295,189 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 via J.P. Morgan Securities at a weighted average price of 389.66 pence per share, spending approximately £1.15m. The shares will be cancelled. Following the transaction, 393,963,764 ordinary shares remain in issue with total voting rights of 393,963,764.
17699352.brief: Pantheon International repurchased 295,189 shares at 388-390.5p on 21 July; plans cancellation.
17699352.take: Share buyback with cancellation typically signals management confidence in valuation and returns capital to remaining shareholders; modest buyback size suggests measured capital allocation approach.

17699350.summary: Talon Resources' Phase 1 exploration at Eagle Lake, Ontario returned strong gold results including 1.85m @ 7.17g/t Au and 4.80m @ 4.47g/t Au at East Fornieri Bay, plus 32.90g/t Au grab sample at Parker Shear. A new Moss Knoll target was identified, with 8.00m @ 0.67g/t Au. Results will be integrated with historical data and machine learning targeting to refine drill targets for a planned 1,000m maiden drilling programme in Q4 2026. The project covers 1,986 hectares with Talon holding a 90% interest.
17699350.brief: Talon Resources confirms high-grade gold across Eagle Lake targets; maiden drilling planned Q4 2026.
17699350.take: Early-stage exploration success with visible gold confirmation across multiple targets strengthens pre-drill confidence; Q4 drilling will provide first systematic test of discovery potential, likely supporting positive sentiment for exploration-stage investors.

17699361.summary: Gaming Realms PLC purchased 29,094 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 via Peel Hunt at prices ranging from 29.90p to 30.50p, with a VWAP of 30.13p. The shares will be held in treasury. The company now holds 24,393,381 treasury shares with 271,872,633 shares in issue, representing total voting rights.
17699361.brief: Gaming Realms repurchased 29,094 shares at 29.90-30.50p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
17699361.take: Routine share buyback execution within an announced programme; supports capital management but signals no major strategic developments or material news.

17699363.summary: RentGuarantor Holdings (AIM: RGG) received notice of warrant exercise for 1,080,000 ordinary shares at 17.5p per share, generating approximately £189,000 in gross proceeds. Following admission to AIM on 23 July 2026, total issued ordinary share capital will be 149,872,459 shares with one voting right each. The company holds no treasury shares.
17699363.brief: RentGuarantor exercises 1.08m warrants, raising £189k; share count rises to 149.9m.
17699363.take: Routine warrant exercise with modest capital raise; shareholder dilution offset by cash injection; non-event for market.

17699364.summary: Q3 revenues reached £4.1m, bringing nine-month total to £8.9m, ahead of prior guidance. Full-year performance now expected materially ahead of previous expectations (prior guidance: £13.4m revenues, £4.3m EBITDA). Board now confident of doubling revenues in current financial year and more than doubling again within 2-3 years. £4.5m capital investment approved for three new coating reactors operational in latter part of 2027. Company secured ~50% of anticipated 2027 tungsten gas requirements, mitigating input cost inflation.
17699364.brief: Hardide trading update shows strong momentum with FY revenues tracking ahead of expectations; £4.5m capacity investment approved.
17699364.take: Strong operational momentum and upgraded guidance with visible investment backing growth ambitions should support positive sentiment, though execution risk on capacity delivery and Middle East/semi-conductor expansion remains.

17699366.summary: International Workplace Group plc purchased 153,656 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at volume-weighted average price of 1.9462 GBP across multiple venues (LSE, CBOE, Turquoise, Aquis). Since programme launch 31 December 2025, the company has acquired 40,316,339 shares. Post-purchase, 953,972,226 shares remain in issue excluding treasury stock. Shares will be cancelled or held in treasury per shareholder authority from May 2026 AGM.
17699366.brief: IWG repurchased 153,656 shares on 21 July as part of ongoing buyback programme; 40.3m shares bought since December 2025.
17699366.take: Routine capital management execution within disclosed authority. Share buybacks can support EPS but market reaction depends on valuation views and broader company fundamentals not addressed here.

17699362.summary: On 21 July 2026, Grafton Group plc repurchased 60,000 ordinary shares at a volume-weighted average price of £9.4124 through Goodbody Stockbrokers on the London Stock Exchange. The shares are being cancelled as part of the £25m buyback programme announced 30 June 2026. To date, 959,172 shares have been purchased in aggregate through Goodbody and Deutsche Bank since programme commencement.
17699362.brief: Grafton Group purchases 60,000 shares at £9.41 average price as part of £25m buyback programme.
17699362.take: Routine capital management execution; buyback in progress as planned. No material change in direction or strategic significance—standard shareholder return mechanism.

17699329.summary: Infection prevention company Tristel delivered FY2026 revenues of £51.1m (up 10% from £46.5m) with adjusted PBT expected at £11.5m (14% growth; FY2025: £10.1m), marginally above market consensus of £11.4m. Adjusted EBITDA margins remained above the 25% target. The company maintained its debt-free status with cash balances of £16.0m (up from £12.8m), supporting overseas expansion. New CEO Chris Lee joins 1 August 2026.
17699329.brief: Tristel posts 10% revenue growth to £51.1m and 14% adjusted PBT growth to £11.5m for FY2026, meeting guidance.
17699329.take: Solid execution on stated targets with double-digit revenue and profit growth and strong cash generation likely supports investor confidence. Ahead of consensus and maintaining margin discipline suggests sustainable business model.

17699330.summary: Firering Strategic Minerals (AIM: FRG) has hot commissioned Kiln 3 at its Limeco quicklime operation in Zambia on 20 July 2026. With three kilns now operational, combined production is expected to exceed 200 tonnes per day, positioning Limeco as one of Zambia's largest high-quality lime producers. Firering increased its stake from 41.7% to 45% following completion of final option payments. The operation is transitioning from breakeven to cash flow positive, expected to be profitable by year-end.
17699330.brief: Firering's Limeco lime project commissions third kiln, transitioning to profitability with 200+ tpd output target.
17699330.take: Production ramp-up and margin inflection are material positives, supported by increased ownership stake signalling management confidence. Market likely views this as validation of the Limeco thesis with clear path to earnings contribution.

17699331.summary: Ariana Resources (9.9% stake in Turkish operator Zenit) reports successful completion of Tavşan mine ramp-up with ore loading at specification rate of 4,000 tonnes daily onto heap-leach pads. Gold recovery maintained at ~70% via ADR circuit with ~350,000 tonnes currently leaching. Recent diamond drilling shows potential pit extensions; 750,000 tonnes ore stockpiled across grades. Kiziltepe sector interests transferred into separate legal entity via in specie distribution to allow independent future transactions; Ariana pursuing sale of its 9.9% Kiziltepe stake to Proccea Construction Co.
17699331.brief: Tavşan mine ramp-up complete at 4,000 tonnes/day; Kiziltepe interests separated for potential sale.
17699331.take: Positive operational momentum with Tavşan progressing on schedule and improved processing efficiency, though market impact limited by Ariana's minority stake and complexity of multi-party Turkish transactions.

17699327.summary: Sirius Real Estate has completed acquisition of a 57,771 sqm light-industrial business park in Fulda, Germany for €49.8 million. The fully-let asset generates €3.93 million annual rent with 5.1 year weighted average lease expiry and 7.8% EPRA Net Initial Yield. The anchor tenant, a European ballistic protection manufacturer serving military and law enforcement, has occupied the site since 2014 and benefits from structural defence spending growth, including German Armed Forces' MOBAST programme supporting multi-year production visibility.
17699327.brief: Sirius Real Estate completes €49.8m acquisition of fully-let German business park in Fulda with 7.8% yield.
17699327.take: Positive acquisition signal: attractive 7.8% yield on fully-let asset with government-backed secular demand tailwinds and long-term tenant commitment supports dividend progression prospects.

17699325.summary: Potter & Moore plc has posted its Annual Report and Accounts for the year ended 31 March 2026 to shareholders, with copies available on its investor website. The company's Annual General Meeting will be held at 11:00 a.m. on Thursday 20 August 2026 at its registered office in Peterborough. The announcement includes contact details for shareholder enquiries and advisers Zeus.
17699325.brief: Potter & Moore posts FY26 Annual Report and AGM notice; meeting scheduled for 20 August 2026.
17699325.take: Routine regulatory filing with no material content disclosed; standard corporate governance activity with no operational or financial substance to influence sentiment.

17699324.summary: M.P. Evans Group harvested 705,400 tonnes of fresh fruit bunches in H1 2026, up 14% from 619,100 tonnes in H1 2025, with own crops rising 13% and scheme-smallholder crops up 15%. CPO production increased 11% to 192,300 tonnes and palm kernels rose 11% to 42,100 tonnes. Mill-gate pricing averaged US$873/tonne for CPO (up from US$868 in H1 2025) and US$813/tonne for PK (up 9% from US$747), demonstrating strong commodity pricing alongside volume growth.
17699324.brief: M.P. Evans reports H1 2026 crop up 14% and CPO production up 11% year-on-year; pricing remains robust.
17699324.take: Strong operational performance with double-digit volume growth, improved extraction rates, and robust commodity pricing should support earnings; declining reliance on purchased crops improves margins, signalling positive trajectory for shareholders.

17699323.summary: Air Astana JSC, Central Asia's largest airline group by revenue and fleet size, will release second quarter and half-year 2026 financial results on Wednesday, 5 August 2026. Management will host a live webcast and Q&A conference call at 10:00 BST (14:00 Astana time) available in English, Kazakh, and Russian. The Group operates 63 aircraft through Air Astana and its low-cost subsidiary FlyArystan, serving domestic, regional and international routes.
17699323.brief: Air Astana announces Q2 and H1 2026 results on 5 August 2026 with webcast in three languages.
17699323.take: Routine results announcement notice; no material information disclosed. Market will await actual financial figures on 5 August to assess operational and financial performance.

17699321.summary: Banco Santander S.A. published its first half 2026 results on 22 July 2026, comprising a financial report and earnings presentation. The documents have been submitted to the FCA's National Storage Mechanism and are available for inspection. The linked document content could not be accessed due to technical limitations, preventing retrieval of specific financial metrics or performance figures.
17699321.brief: Banco Santander releases 1H2026 financial results and earnings presentation documents.
17699321.take: Routine disclosure of interim results; market impact depends on underlying earnings data not accessible in this announcement, making sentiment assessment premature without reviewing full reports.

17699320.summary: Banco Santander reported underlying profit of €7.328bn in H1 2026, up 15% year-on-year, driven by 12 million new customers (182m total), revenue growth of 6% to €30.8bn, and cost discipline from ONE Transformation programme. Net interest income rose 7% and net fee income 9%. The bank completed the TSB acquisition on 30 April, adding 4+ million UK customers and £400m+ expected cost synergies. CET1 ratio stands at 14.0%. Santander plans €1.8bn share buyback against H1 results (ECB-approved) and reiterates 2026 targets including mid-single-digit revenue growth and CET1 ratio of 12.8-13%.
17699320.brief: Santander posts record H1 2026 underlying profit of €7.3bn, up 15%; completes TSB acquisition; on track for 2026 targets.
17699320.take: Strong operational execution and customer growth, offset by M&A integration risks and geopolitical uncertainties. Market likely reads positively on profitability beat and capital returns, though TSB integration execution bears monitoring.

17699301.summary: IADB priced GBP 50m of 5.000% fixed-rate notes due July 20, 2030 at 101.960% plus accrued interest on July 17, 2026. This eighth tranche consolidates with seven prior tranches totaling GBP 1.1bn under Series 877, all bearing identical terms. Notes admitted to LSE UK Main Market; Citigroup is dealer. Proceeds enter ordinary capital resources for development lending across Latin America and Caribbean.
17699301.brief: Inter-American Development Bank issues GBP 50m tranche of 5% notes due 2030, consolidating into existing series.
17699301.take: Routine multilateral development bank debt issuance to existing liabilities; consolidation indicates strong investor demand for this series but represents incremental funding, not new strategic direction.

17699304.summary: Resolute Mining's updated mineral resource estimate for the 100%-owned ABC Project in northwest Côte d'Ivoire shows 132.7 million tonnes grading 0.71 g/t gold for 3.0 million ounces (inferred, at 0.3 g/t cut-off). This represents an expansion from the previous 2.16 Moz following 31,000m of drilling since acquisition in 2025. The company will invest $15-25 million over 12-18 months on an 80,000m infill drill programme to convert resources to indicated category, with feasibility studies and mining application targeted by end-2027. Mineralisation remains open at depth and along strike.
17699304.brief: Resolute Mining expands ABC Project mineral resource to 3.0 Moz gold in Côte d'Ivoire, targeting fourth West African mine.
17699304.take: Market likely views this positively as meaningful resource growth at an early-stage project with clear development pathway and low cut-off grades supporting future mine viability; demonstrates exploration success and adds to Resolute's West African diversification strategy.

17699332.summary: Rockwood Strategic PLC agreed on 21 July 2026 to issue 125,000 ordinary shares of 5p each at 319.89p per share from its blocklisting facility, with cash settlement on 23 July 2026. Post-issuance, the Company will have 60,768,760 issued ordinary shares with no treasury holdings, establishing the voting rights denominator for FCA disclosure notifications.
17699332.brief: Rockwood Strategic issues 125,000 shares at 319.89p per share for cash via blocklisting facility.
17699332.take: Routine capital raise via established facility; modest share dilution (~0.2%) suggests incremental funding requirement rather than strategic signal, likely market non-event.

17699358.summary: On 21 July 2026, Celebrus Technologies (AIM: CLBS) purchased 24,334 ordinary shares of 2p each at a volume-weighted average price of 107.49p through Cavendish Capital Markets. The shares are held in treasury. Following the transaction, the company has 40,431,453 shares in issue with 2,590,629 in treasury, reducing voting rights to 37,840,824 shares.
17699358.brief: Celebrus Technologies repurchases 24,334 shares at 107.49p per share for treasury.
17699358.take: Routine share buyback announcement; modest scale suggests opportunistic treasury building rather than major capital deployment. Standard corporate housekeeping with no clear strategic signal.

17699335.summary: Liontrust completed its acquisition of River Global Holdings on 30 June 2026, adding £3.0bn in AuMA and bringing total assets under management and advice to £24.3bn (from £19.6bn on 31 March 2026). The group reported net outflows of £357m in Q1 FY2026, improving from £1.1bn in the prior year, with £520m gross institutional inflows. Twenty-nine funds ranked in the first or second quartile over three years, including eight River Global funds, supporting management's assertion of strengthened investment capabilities and distribution positioning.
17699335.brief: Liontrust AuMA hits £24.3bn post-River Global acquisition; net outflows of £357m in quarter.
17699335.take: Market likely views positively: transformational acquisition doubles AuMA, dramatically improves flow trend, and adds high-performing funds, though outflows suggest ongoing competitive pressure in retail channels.

17699336.summary: Gamma Communications purchased 15,755 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 through Investec at prices ranging from 943.0p to 951.5p, with a weighted average of 947.56p. The company has now repurchased 2,762,818 shares under its buyback programme announced 13 January 2026 and intends to cancel them. Post-settlement, 91,001,533 shares remain in issue with 89,497,859 voting rights (excluding 1,503,674 treasury shares). The announcement was made under Takeover Code Rule 2.9 due to an offer period commenced 7 April 2026.
17699336.brief: Gamma Communications repurchased 15,755 shares on 21 July 2026 at 943-951.5p as part of its January 2026 buyback programme.
17699336.take: Routine buyback update under an existing programme during an active offer period. No material new information; demonstrates steady capital returns but non-event for investors given transparent, scheduled nature.

17699337.summary: Norcros, the UK and Ireland's leading branded bathroom products business, reported 3.1% constant currency like-for-like revenue growth for the 13-week period to 5 July 2026. On a reported basis, revenue was 27.9% higher, reflecting the Fibo acquisition completed in October 2025. The group achieved market share gains and price increases across both regions, offsetting weak underlying market demand. CEO Thomas Willcocks stated the group made a solid start to the year with performance in line with expectations, and the board maintains its full-year guidance unchanged.
17699337.brief: Norcros reports solid Q1 trading with 3.1% like-for-like revenue growth; full-year outlook unchanged.
17699337.take: Solid but uninspiring Q1 performance affirms guidance; modest like-for-like growth offset by acquisition contribution suggests underlying market remains soft, likely read as modestly positive but cautious.

17699338.summary: GlobalData PLC purchased 91,029 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 through Investec at prices ranging from 73.40p to 74.50p, with a weighted average price of 74.1459p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing total issued shares to 745,518,757 and total voting rights accordingly. This forms part of the Share Buyback Programme that commenced 23 June 2026.
17699338.brief: GlobalData repurchased 91,029 shares at 73.4-74.5p as part of its ongoing buyback programme.
17699338.take: Routine share buyback execution reducing share count; typical capital allocation management signalling management confidence, though modest scale suggests incremental approach rather than aggressive capital return.

17699339.summary: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited repurchased 40,000 USD-denominated ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at GBP 4.337044 per share, to be held as treasury shares. Total treasury holdings increased to 6,680,142 shares. Following the transaction, voting shares in issue total 120,399,381, which represents the denominator for FCA disclosure threshold calculations.
17699339.brief: VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund repurchased 40,000 shares at GBP 4.34 per share on 21 July 2026.
17699339.take: Routine share buyback announcement with no material impact; typical capital management activity for closed-ended funds seeking to manage share price discount.

17699340.summary: Edinburgh Investment Trust bought 50,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at an average price of 825p per share via Investec Bank on the LSE. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company holds 69.8m shares in treasury out of 195.7m issued shares, leaving 125.8m shares with voting rights.
17699340.brief: Edinburgh Investment Trust purchases 50,000 own shares at 825p each for treasury holding.
17699340.take: Routine share buyback for treasury; typical capital management activity signalling confidence but with no immediate material impact on shareholders or valuation.

17699341.summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 500,000 ordinary shares through Investec Bank plc on 21 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 134.9208p, with a trading range of 134.60p to 135.20p. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this purchase, HICL holds 168.5m treasury shares, with 1.86bn shares in issue excluding treasury stock, representing the total voting rights denominator for disclosure purposes.
17699341.brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchased 500,000 shares at 134.92p average price on 21 July 2026.
17699341.take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and capital efficiency, though this is a modest repurchase that may be part of a regular programme. Market likely treats as neutral to mildly positive.

17699342.summary: MESCT delivered NAV total return of 1.8% and share price return of 1.5% for year ended 31 March 2026, underperforming MSCI Europe ex-UK SmallCap benchmark's 17.5% return. Despite near-term underperformance, 10-year NAV total return reached 177.8%, outperforming benchmark by 41.1%. Revenue per share rose 29.3% to 1.94p; total dividends increased 18.3% to 1.90p plus 0.35p special dividend. Company executed 52.6m share buybacks contributing 3.4% NAV uplift; net gearing rose to 5.1% from 2.1%. Portfolio fundamentals remain robust with annualised EPS growth of 15.4% over past decade.
17699342.brief: Montanaro European Smaller Companies Trust posts 1.8% NAV return for FY2026, significantly lagging 17.5% benchmark amid persistent quality/growth style headwinds.
17699342.take: Significant underperformance versus benchmark in FY2026 driven by style factors rather than stock selection, which remained positive over 3-5-10 years. Strong long-term track record and improving dividends offset by near-term headwinds and modest NAV discount widening to 8.7%. Market likely views this as cyclical drawdown in quality-growth factors rather than fundamental manager failure.

17699343.summary: Mkango Resources received an exercise notice from a warrant holder to convert 550,000 warrants into common shares at 7 pence per share. Upon admission to AIM expected around 27 July 2026, the company's issued share capital will reach 387,803,618 shares. The new warrant shares will rank pari passu with existing shares and be listed on both AIM and TSX-V, with no treasury shares held.
17699343.brief: Mkango Resources exercises 550,000 warrants at 7p per share, issuing new equity.
17699343.take: Routine equity issuance from warrant exercise; modest dilution (~0.14%) but demonstrates investor confidence in converting positions. Non-event for retail investors absent broader share price implications.

17699344.summary: HarbourVest Global Private Equity Limited purchased 7,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at an average price of £33.1514 per share for cancellation, exercising authority granted at its 15 July AGM. Following the transaction, share capital stands at 69,604,352 shares in issue. The repurchase represents a modest capital management action for the FTSE 250 listed closed-end fund.
17699344.brief: HarbourVest repurchased 7,000 shares at £33.15 each for cancellation under AGM authority.
17699344.take: Routine share buyback under existing mandate; modest scale suggests measured capital return rather than aggressive consolidation; no material market implications.

17699345.summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited purchased 16,600 ordinary shares at 164.00 pence per share on 21 July 2026 via AIM, to be held in treasury. The cumulative buyback total under the programme announced 4 March 2026 now stands at 5,279,700 shares. Following this purchase, total issued share capital is 137,125,806 shares, with voting rights of 131,846,106 shares after treasury holdings.
17699345.brief: AdvancedAdvT continues share buyback, purchasing 16,600 shares at 164p on 21 July.
17699345.take: Routine buyback execution under previously announced programme; consistent with capital management strategy but provides no material new information to market.

17699346.summary: M.P. Evans Group executed a share buyback on 21 July 2026, purchasing 2,316 shares at a volume-weighted average price of 1,678.18 pence per share through Cavendish Capital Markets. The shares were cancelled, reducing total shares in issue to 52,155,249. The buyback prices ranged from 1,662p to 1,696p per share, traded on AIM.
17699346.brief: M.P. Evans repurchased 2,316 shares for cancellation on 21 July at prices between 1,662-1,696p.
17699346.take: Routine capital management action; modest buyback scale suggests measured shareholder return rather than aggressive value signal. Likely neutral market reception.

17699347.summary: Caledonia Investments plc purchased 120,947 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 through Peel Hunt LLP at a weighted average price of 378.26 pence, with prices ranging from 377.00p to 380.00p. The shares will be cancelled, reducing issued share capital to 512,089,373 ordinary shares with voting rights. This is a routine capital management transaction.
17699347.brief: Caledonia Investments repurchased 120,947 own shares at average 378.26p per share for cancellation.
17699347.take: Share buyback at modest scale suggests steady capital management; likely read as neutral shareholder return activity with no material strategic implications.

17699348.summary: The Renewables Infrastructure Group Limited purchased 265,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 74.4867p through Investec Bank plc, under its share buyback programme announced August 2024. The shares will be held as treasury stock. TRIG now holds 153,972,374 treasury shares, with 2,331,990,512 voting rights outstanding excluding treasury shares.
17699348.brief: TRIG repurchases 265,000 shares at 74.49p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
17699348.take: Routine buyback execution under disclosed programme; modest share count reduction supports earnings per share but signals management confidence at current valuation levels.

17699349.summary: Herald Investment Trust completed a tender offer on 9 July 2026, with 60.1% of shares tendered following shareholder resolution of a dispute with 31% shareholder Saba Capital. NAV per share rose 28.1% in H1 2026 to 3,459.4p, driven primarily by AI-related holdings. Post-tender NAV is approximately £650m. Investment manager Katie Potts and HIML team transfer to Aberdeen Investments as the new AIFM on 1 August 2026, maintaining the same investment strategy focused on small/micro-cap tech stocks.
17699349.brief: Herald Investment Trust resolves shareholder dispute; 60% of shares tendered at NAV. Katie Potts remains as manager under new AIFM Aberdeen.
17699349.take: Positive read: dispute resolution removes uncertainty, long-term shareholders demonstrated commitment by not tendering, and continuity of successful management structure preserved under larger institutional backing (Aberdeen) provides scale benefits.

17699354.summary: Sealand Capital Galaxy (LSE: SCGL) has conditionally agreed to acquire 100% of Brilliant Glow Group Co., Limited for £6.6 million. BGG, established in 2020 and based in Hong Kong/Shenzhen, operates a team of ~10 professionals providing digital transformation, technology, retail and capital markets consulting across APAC. A £1.98m deposit was paid in March 2026; the remaining £4.62m balance is due within one year post-completion and may be satisfied in cash or shares at Sealand's discretion. A two-year performance commitment requires BGG to achieve £2m cumulative revenue or full realisation of equity awards, with seller repurchase rights at 110% of paid consideration if targets are missed.
17699354.brief: Sealand Capital Galaxy to acquire Hong Kong consulting firm Brilliant Glow Group for £6.6m to expand APAC capabilities.
17699354.take: Small strategic bolt-on acquisition aligned with stated APAC expansion strategy; modest size relative to typical LSE companies; downside protected via repurchase clause, but execution risk on integrating team and converting client contracts into revenue.

17699333.summary: The AIM-listed flooring manufacturer expects FY2026 sales marginally below prior year with profit down 10-15% versus FY2025. Resilient sheet flooring remained solid, but luxury vinyl tile faced headwinds in Europe and Australia. Management absorbed Iran conflict-driven input cost surcharges to protect contractor relationships and gain market share. UK sales modestly grew despite budget pressures in education, healthcare and hospitality. Strong growth continued in North America (54% ahead), Middle East, and South Africa (25% ahead), while Europe remained muted. Strong balance sheet with no debt supports medium-term confidence.
17699333.brief: James Halstead warns of modest sales decline and 10-15% profit drop for FY2026 due to LVT headwinds and Iran-linked cost pressures.
17699333.take: Profit warning of 10-15% combined with modest sales decline likely negative; offset partly by strong regional performance and cost absorption strategy showing management prudence, but geopolitical and demand headwinds signal challenging outlook.

17699457.summary: Pershing Square Holdings repurchased 35,360 public shares on 21 July 2026 at an average price of $50.68 USD ($3,788 pence). The buyback comprised 24,047 shares on PSH ticker (average $50.67) and 11,313 on PSHD ticker (average $50.70). NAV per share stood at $77.06 as of 14 July. Post-transaction, PSH has 173.7 million shares outstanding with 37.2 million held in treasury.
17699457.brief: PSH repurchased 35,360 shares at average $50.68 on 21 July 2026 across LSE venues.
17699457.take: Routine share buyback reducing share count at prices below stated NAV, typically supporting per-share value for remaining shareholders; standard capital management for closed-ended funds.

17699473.summary: Andrew Quinn, aged 72, joins Cornish Metals' board effective 22 July 2026 as independent Non-Executive Director and Audit Committee member. Quinn is a chartered engineer and former CIBC mining investment banking head with extensive board experience at ASX-listed Alkane Resources, Barrick Mining, and LBMA. He brings capital markets, M&A and governance expertise as the company advances South Crofty towards production as Europe/North America's first primary tin producer. Quinn holds no current shares in the company.
17699473.brief: Cornish Metals appoints Andrew Quinn, seasoned mining executive with 50+ years experience, as independent Non-Executive Director.
17699473.take: Positive appointment signalling serious development progress; Quinn's mining pedigree and board-level experience lends credibility during transition to production phase, likely reassuring investors and stakeholders.

17699456.summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited repurchased 100,000 Class A Shares on London Stock Exchange on 21 July 2026 at prices between £14.25 and £14.38 per share, pursuant to shareholder authority granted 11 June 2026. All shares will be cancelled, reducing outstanding Class A Shares to 40,514,149 from the previous level. The company also holds 3,150,408 Class A shares in treasury.
17699456.brief: NBPE bought back 100,000 Class A shares on 21 July 2026 at £14.25-£14.38, which will be cancelled.
17699456.take: Routine capital management activity; share cancellation reduces share count and can support per-share metrics, though modest buyback volume suggests non-event for near-term sentiment.

17699472.summary: Capita has issued $55m (£41m net) of unsecured senior notes via US Private Placement with a three-year maturity and 7.54% GBP coupon. Proceeds will refinance existing debt and fund general corporate purposes. The company simultaneously amended its interest cover ratio covenant on existing US Private Placement notes to a 3.0x-3.5x range, aligning with its new revolving credit facility to provide flexibility during its transformation.
17699472.brief: Capita issues $55m US Private Placement Notes at 7.54% coupon for debt refinancing and working capital.
17699472.take: Refinancing move signals debt management and liquidity planning; covenant amendment suggests lenders accommodating restructuring efforts. Likely neutral-to-positive as it demonstrates capital market access and improved covenant consistency.

17700145.summary: 88 Energy's South Prudhoe project gross unrisked 2U prospective resources increased ~35% to 768.9 MMbbls (640.7 MMbbls net to 88E). Augusta-1 exploration well targeting three reservoirs (Ivishak, Kuparuk, Upper Schrader Bluff) with 133.7 MMbbls gross unrisked 2U resources is planned for Q1 2027 spud, contingent on funding and permitting. Nordic Rig-3 and Arctic-rated camp secured. Company held A$8.2 million cash at quarter-end; Namibia PEL 93 farm-in amended reducing capital exposure by ~US$15 million while securing unconditional 20% working interest.
17700145.brief: 88 Energy advances Alaska exploration with upgraded South Prudhoe resources and secured drilling rig for 2027 campaign.
17700145.take: Material resource upgrade and operational progress on flagship Alaska project supports exploration narrative, though 2027 spud remains dependent on securing funding and farm-out completion—execution risk remains significant for near-term catalysts.

17699648.summary: On 21 July 2026, IG Group transferred 2,100,000 ordinary shares from treasury to its Employee Benefit Trust at nil consideration to satisfy awards under the Long Term Incentive Plan 2023 and Sustained Performance Plan. Following the transfer, issued share capital stands at 361.6m shares, with 28.9m in treasury and 332.6m carrying voting rights. The transfer represents routine equity incentive plan administration.
17699648.brief: IG Group transfers 2.1m treasury shares to employee benefit trust for long-term incentive plan awards.
17699648.take: Routine equity incentive plan execution with no material market implications. Share dilution is pre-existing via treasury reserves rather than new issuance.

17699645.summary: Talon Resources (90% owner of Wedgetail Mining, holder of Eagle Lake project) completed Phase 1 exploration in Ontario, Canada. Channel sampling confirmed gold mineralisation across multiple targets with highlights including 1.85m @ 7.17g/t Au and 4.80m @ 4.47g/t Au at East Fornieri Bay, plus a new Moss Knoll target identified. A 1,000m maiden diamond drilling programme is planned for Q4 2026, with results to be integrated with machine learning targeting. Gunsynd holds the remaining 10% interest in the project.
17699645.brief: Gunsynd's 10% stake in Eagle Lake gold project shows promising Phase 1 results ahead of Q4 2026 drilling.
17699645.take: Early-stage exploration success de-risks the project and supports confidence in imminent drilling, likely viewed positively by junior exploration investors, though Gunsynd's minority stake limits upside leverage compared to Talon.

17699423.summary: Skillcast Group PLC (AIM: SKL) reported H1 2026 revenues of £8.2m, up 10% year-on-year, driven by recurring subscription revenues which grew 15% to £7.4m and now represent 90% of total revenues. ARR increased 14% over 12 months to £14.5m and 5% since year-start. The Group maintains no debt with £13.6m cash (15p per share). Profit margins continue improving through operational gearing and AI productivity gains. Full interim results due 30 September 2026.
17699423.brief: Skillcast reports H1 2026 revenues of £8.2m (+10%), with ARR up 14% to £14.5m and strong cash position.
17699423.take: Solid organic growth with improving margins and strong cash generation, though CEO notes political/economic uncertainty is slowing customer decision-making; likely read as positive execution within guidance but tempered by macro headwinds.

17699427.summary: Cambridge Raman Imaging (26.81% owned by Frontier IP) received a €400,000 European Innovation Council Transition grant for scaling its Raman imaging technology. CRI signed a distribution agreement with Hooke Instruments to enter the Chinese market and announced a strategic pivot toward pharmaceutical industrial quality control, where the global market is valued at $5 billion and forecast to reach $13 billion by 2033. The company is in discussions with two leading pharmaceutical groups for pilot testing.
17699427.brief: Frontier IP portfolio company Cambridge Raman Imaging wins €400k EU grant, signs China distribution deal, targets pharma quality control.
17699427.take: Non-dilutive EU funding and concrete pharma sector traction suggest CRI is progressing toward commercialisation with lower regulatory barriers than medical markets, potentially accelerating value realisation for Frontier IP's stake.

17699425.summary: Transense Technologies has appointed Hamaton Ltd and sister company Tyresure Ltd as authorised distributors for Translogik tyre inspection devices and TireTask digital management platform across Europe and the Middle East. Hamaton provides established European logistics infrastructure, regional warehousing and multilingual support for the TPMS brand. The appointment targets growing regulatory demand from mandatory TPMS fitment on commercial vehicles, Digital Product Passport requirements, and RFID-enabled tyre adoption. Transense retains direct sales capability and flexibility to appoint additional partners.
17699425.brief: Transense appoints Hamaton and Tyresure as European distributors for Translogik tyre inspection products and TireTask software.
17699425.take: Positive move expanding international reach through established regional partner with existing fleet and aftermarket relationships, capitalising on European regulatory tailwinds without capital-intensive direct operations.

17699424.summary: GreenX Metals has begun active exploration at its Eleonore North Project in East Greenland, focusing on gold, tungsten, and antimony mineralisation. A RIRGS specialist will evaluate the Noa Pluton gold-antimony prospect and untested targets. Bulk sampling of tungsten and antimony material at North and South Margeries deposits will support metallurgical sighter test work. Archive core samples are being assayed with results expected in coming months. Tungsten and antimony are EU and US-designated critical raw materials with global supply concentrated in China; the project hosts high-grade occurrences in a stable Western jurisdiction.
17699424.brief: GreenX Metals commences summer fieldwork at Eleonore North project in East Greenland targeting gold, tungsten and antimony.
17699424.take: Early-stage exploration progress at a frontier project with exposure to supply-constrained critical materials; positive strategic positioning but metallurgical results remain pending and drilling decisions dependent on sighter test work outcomes.

17699422.summary: HgCapital Trust PLC, via manager Hg, is investing approximately £7 million in Street Group alongside other Hg institutional clients through the Hg Mercury Fund. Street Group, founded by siblings Tom and Heather Staff, values the company at over £200 million. The business provides integrated software and AI tools for UK estate and letting agents, including its CRM platform Street.co.uk, Spectre prospecting tool, and AI-native Cortex product. The founders retain majority control and will continue leading operations. HgT's available liquid resources total £278 million (12% of NAV), with outstanding commitments reducing to £2.1 billion.
17699422.brief: HgCapital Trust invests £7m in Street Group, a Manchester-based PropTech software platform for UK estate agents, at £200m+ valuation.
17699422.take: Positive signal: deployment of capital into high-growth PropTech with AI focus aligns with Hg's stated strategy; demonstrates active investment activity and confidence in emerging AI applications, though represents modest capital deployment relative to HgT's size.

17699421.summary: Primary Health Properties PLC, the UK's largest listed healthcare REIT following its 2025 merger with Assura, will release interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 on 30 July 2026. The company operates a £6 billion portfolio of 1,142 healthcare properties across the UK and Ireland, generating £342m annual rental income and serving over 10 million patients. A virtual presentation for analysts and investors will be held at 11:30am BST with webcast access and Q&A.
17699421.brief: PHP to announce interim results for H1 2026 on 30 July with analyst presentation and Q&A.
17699421.take: Routine results announcement for a major healthcare infrastructure REIT with strong fundamentals; market likely neutral as this is standard disclosure, though H1 performance details may move sentiment once published.

17699393.summary: Boku Inc (AIM: BOKU) has appointed Peter Klein as Chief Commercial Officer effective 22 July 2026. Klein brings 25+ years' experience in payments and fintech, most recently from Mastercard where he built the global Account-to-Account and Cross-Border Payments business. He will lead Boku's commercial organisation and go-to-market function, reporting to CEO Stuart Neal. The appointment supports growth ambitions as local payment methods are forecast to account for 60%+ of transactions in a $10.6trn global e-commerce market by 2028.
17699393.brief: Boku appoints Peter Klein as Chief Commercial Officer to drive merchant growth and expand its local payments network.
17699393.take: Market likely views this positively as Klein's heavyweight Mastercard and payments sector pedigree signals serious commercial execution capability to capitalise on the high-growth local payments opportunity Boku targets.

17699390.summary: Revenue grew 16% to £177.0m (FY2025: £152.2m) comprising 10% organic and 6% inorganic growth. Adjusted underlying EBITDA rose 12% to £46.1m. Reported profit before tax increased 13% to £35.5m. The firm maintained strong market positions, ranking 16th in UK M&A (up from 19th) and 14% market share in administrations. Net cash stood at £26.2m with no external borrowings. Total dividends increased 7% to 5.8p. Post-year-end, the Group secured £50m committed credit facilities plus £45m accordion capacity through June 2029.
17699390.brief: FRP Advisory delivered 7th consecutive year of profit and revenue growth to £177m, up 16%, with 12% EBITDA rise.
17699390.take: Consistent execution and market share gains in resilient advisory services amid economic uncertainty likely appeal to growth-focused investors, though working capital deployment and reduced net cash warrant monitoring.

17699394.summary: Thomas Shorten, Chief Executive Officer of ProService Building Services Marketplace plc (AIM: PRO), purchased 639,466 ordinary shares on 20-21 July 2026. On 20 July, he bought 363,962 shares at 2.32p average; on 21 July, 275,504 shares at 2.08p average. Total investment: £14,068. Shorten's beneficial holding now totals 1,329,654 shares, including those held by closely associated persons.
17699394.brief: ProService CEO Thomas Shorten purchases 639,466 shares across two days at average price of 2.2p.
17699394.take: Insider buying by the CEO at subdued prices suggests management confidence in value, though modest transaction size limits conviction signal. Market likely treats this as mildly positive sentiment indicator.

17699391.summary: Seeing Machines has won a Driver & Occupant Monitoring System program with a European automotive OEM through an existing Tier 1 customer. The camera-based technology will integrate into rear-view mirror solutions across multiple vehicle platforms including electric vehicles, with production starting in 2028. The award is expected to generate approximately US$5 million in lifetime revenue and comes as EU General Safety Regulation requirements for driver monitoring take effect, positioning the company to benefit from industry transition toward camera-based in-cabin safety systems.
17699391.brief: Seeing Machines secures European automotive DMS/OMS program worth ~US$5m lifetime revenue, integrating into rear-view mirrors from 2028.
17699391.take: Positive signal reinforcing Seeing Machines' market position and technology leadership; regulatory tailwinds and scalable rear-view mirror architecture reduce deployment barriers, supporting pipeline growth and revenue visibility into 2028.

17699392.summary: Great Southern Copper reported channel sampling results from 20 traverses (409m, 214 samples) at Monolith Target, Cerro Negro Prospect, Chile. Key intervals include 56m @ 28.5 g/t Ag & 1,745 ppm Cu, and 78m @ 10.1 g/t Ag & 736 ppm Cu. Results define Cu-Ag mineralisation across 700x400m in altered dacite dome, 400m from the Mostaza discovery. Surface oxidation/leaching suggests higher grades at depth. Company plans geophysical surveys and scout drilling to test the prospect.
17699392.brief: GSC's Monolith target confirms broad Cu-Ag mineralisation over 700x400m, positioning it as priority drill prospect.
17699392.take: Positive indicator of exploration upside; broad surface anomalies and proximity to Mostaza deposit enhance prospectivity, though grades remain surface-level and drill confirmation is needed before major value creation.

17699396.summary: Natalie-Jane Macdonald, Non-Executive Director at Pets at Home Group Plc, purchased 2,525 ordinary shares at £1.9758 per share on 20 July 2026 via the London Stock Exchange, totalling £4,998.86. This is an initial notification of a director shareholding transaction, representing a modest personal investment by a board member.
17699396.brief: Non-Executive Director Natalie-Jane Macdonald purchases 2,525 Pets at Home shares for £4,998.86.
17699396.take: Routine disclosure; director's modest share purchase signals minor confidence but carries minimal market significance given small transaction size.

17699420.summary: Secure Trust Bank PLC will announce interim results for the period ending 30 June 2026 on Thursday 13 August 2026 at 7:00am. CEO Ian Corfield and CFO Rachel Lawrence will host a 10:00am webcast presentation covering half-year trading performance and strategic progress, with an additional shortened presentation via Investor Meet Company at 4:30pm the same day. Both presentations are open to existing and prospective shareholders.
17699420.brief: Secure Trust Bank to release H1 2026 interim results on 13 August with investor presentations scheduled.
17699420.take: Routine announcement of interim results release date and investor meeting arrangements; no material disclosures provided. Market will await actual results on 13 August for substantive content.

17699397.summary: Qadeer Mohammed, Chief Financial Officer of Potter & Moore PLC (AIM:PAM), has informed the Board of his intention to step down and will not seek re-election at the forthcoming Annual General Meeting. He will work a six-month notice period to ensure smooth transition. The Board will commence an external recruitment process to identify his successor. Chairman Paul Forster thanked Mohammed for his four-year contribution.
17699397.brief: Potter & Moore CFO Qadeer Mohammed to resign, working six-month notice period ahead of AGM.
17699397.take: Planned CFO departure with orderly transition process likely viewed as neutral; six-month notice minimises disruption risk, though investor focus will shift to successor calibre and any strategy implications.

17699398.summary: Michel Cataldo, previously Non-Executive Director with 24 years British Army experience and defence sector expertise, becomes Executive Director heading Resilience and Defence effective 20 July 2026. Callum Sommerton, nominated under shareholder Jonathan Swann's relationship agreement (holding >15% post-placing), joins as Non-Executive Director. Sommerton brings CEO experience from Chill Brands Group and battery supply chain regulatory insight relevant to the Company's battery recycling and critical minerals strategy.
17699398.brief: Technology Minerals appoints Michel Cataldo as Executive Director for Defence and Resilience; Callum Sommerton joins as Non-Executive Director.
17699398.take: Positive signal: promotions strengthen execution of Mantle strategy, with dedicated defence-focused leadership and board expertise in battery/regulatory compliance as company transitions from restructuring to commercialisation.

17699399.summary: AMC V Leonardo SARL, associated with non-executive directors Przemysław Głębocki and Jakub Chechelski, purchased 612,000 ordinary shares at 6.86p on 21 July 2026. The Malaccan Holdings Ltd Concert Party now holds 279,425,426 shares, representing 29.61% of issued share capital. DP Poland operates 142 Domino's Pizza locations across Poland and Croatia, plus 70 Pizzeria 105 locations.
17699399.brief: Malaccan Holdings Concert Party increases stake to 29.61% via 612,000 share purchase at 6.86p per share.
17699399.take: Routine disclosure of PDMR shareholding change within concert party structure; incremental stake increase suggests ongoing confidence but represents non-material corporate event.

17699400.summary: Marc Sale, Executive Director and CEO of First Class Metals PLC (LSE: FCM), exercised 795,000 share options on 21 July 2026, awarded to him on 17 July 2022 at an exercise price of 2.0 pence per ordinary share, resulting in aggregate cost of £15,900. The 795,000 new ordinary shares will be admitted to trading on 27 July 2026. Following issuance, the company's total voting rights will be 425,071,349 ordinary shares.
17699400.brief: First Class Metals CEO Marc Sale exercises 795,000 share options at 2.0p per share, investing £15,900.
17699400.take: Executive share option exercise signals management confidence but modest in scale relative to typical CEO holdings; routine PDMR disclosure with no material implications for shareholders.

17699401.summary: On 21 July 2026, Zak McMurray, Non-Executive Director of One Health Group PLC (AIM: OHGR), purchased 39,840 ordinary shares of 0.5p each at 251p per share via the London Stock Exchange. His total beneficial interest is now 53,173 shares, representing approximately 0.38% of the company's issued share capital. One Health is a profitable, dividend-paying NHS surgical provider treating ~19,000 patients annually across five regions, with FY26 revenue of £31.6m (+11%) and adjusted EBITDA of £2.58m (+28%).
17699401.brief: Non-Executive Director Zak McMurray purchases 39,840 shares at 251p, increasing his holding to 0.38% of issued capital.
17699401.take: Director buying into own company signals confidence in growth strategy and recent strong FY26 results; positive signal but immaterial shareholding size and routine insider purchase mean limited market impact.

17699403.summary: Roaring Waters Capital, Dublin-based entity, crossed a major shareholding threshold in Poolbeg Pharma PLC on 21 July 2026. The investor now holds 50,000,000 voting rights representing 6.55% of the company's voting capital, up from a previous position of 5.74%. The notification was filed on the same date the threshold was crossed.
17699403.brief: Roaring Waters Capital notifies 6.55% stake acquisition in Poolbeg Pharma PLC.
17699403.take: Routine regulatory disclosure of shareholding above notifiable threshold; no material transaction context provided, suggesting routine accumulation rather than strategic announcement.

17699428.summary: Oil and gas exploration company Sintana Energy (TSX-V: SEI, AIM: SEI, OTCQX: SEUSF) will host a live management update presentation on August 6, 2026 at 11:00 EDT/16:00 BST featuring CEO Robert Bose and President Eytan Uliel. The presentation follows the company's Annual General Meeting and is designed to provide existing and potential shareholders unable to attend the AGM with direct access to management. Investors can submit questions pre-event or during the live session via the Investor Meet Company platform.
17699428.brief: Sintana Energy to hold shareholder presentation via Investor Meet Company on August 6, 2026 following AGM.
17699428.take: Routine investor engagement activity; non-event announcement providing no material operational or financial updates. Demonstrates standard shareholder communication practices for a geographically dispersed investor base.

17699407.summary: Tungsten West PLC granted conditional options to directors under its shareholder-approved LTIP on 21 July 2026. CEO Jeffery Court received 75.7m awards (60.5m at 3p, 15.1m at 18p); CFO Philip Povey received 37.8m awards (30.3m at 3p, 7.6m at 18p); Non-Executive Chairman Stephen Harrison received 30.3m awards at 3p. Approximately 20% of CEO and CFO awards carry 'stretch' performance targets significantly exceeding base case assumptions. Options lapse by 15 July 2041 unless earlier vesting conditions are met.
17699407.brief: Tungsten West grants LTIP options to three directors totalling 143.7m shares at 3p and 18p exercise prices.
17699407.take: Routine director remuneration disclosure aligned with shareholder-approved plan. Options vesting tied to production milestones for tungsten/tin mine restart; material only if Hemerdon project faces delays or abandonment.

17699413.summary: Gem Diamonds Limited will publish its half-year 2026 trading update on 28 July 2026 for the six-month period ending 30 June 2026. The company, which owns 70% of the Letšeng mine in Lesotho—the world's highest dollar per carat kimberlite diamond mine—continues focus on large, exceptional white diamonds. In June 2025, Gem Diamonds relinquished its Ghaghoo mining license in Botswana with no further obligations.
17699413.brief: Gem Diamonds to release H1 2026 trading update on 28 July covering January-June period.
17699413.take: Routine disclosure notification of upcoming results. Market will await actual H1 figures on 28 July to assess operational performance and diamond pricing trends in current market conditions.

17699414.summary: Zotefoams PLC, a world leader in high-performance foams, will announce interim results for the six months to 30 June 2026 on 5 August 2026. An analyst meeting via Zoom begins at 9:30am BST. A separate investor presentation via Investor Meet Company platform follows at 1:30pm BST, featuring Group CEO Ronan Cox and Group CFO Nick Wright, open to existing and potential shareholders. Investors can pre-submit questions until 4 August 9:00am BST.
17699414.brief: Zotefoams to report H1 2026 interim results on 5 August with analyst presentation and investor roadshow.
17699414.take: Routine trading update announcement with structured investor engagement; market will focus on actual H1 results rather than this procedural notice.

17699415.summary: ImmuPharma has appointed Thermo Fisher Scientific's Patheon pharma services as drug product CDMO for its Kapiglucagon glucagon prodrug program following a competitive tender. This CMC milestone supports progression toward IND submission and first-in-human studies. The selection follows Bachem AG's appointment as API manufacturing partner. ImmuPharma is evaluating a 505(b)(2) regulatory pathway in the US, potentially reducing development timeline and costs versus a full NDA by leveraging existing glucagon data.
17699415.brief: ImmuPharma selects Thermo Fisher as CDMO partner for Kapiglucagon Type 1 diabetes program.
17699415.take: De-risks development pathway by securing established manufacturing partner and pursuing accelerated 505(b)(2) approval route; positive signal on program momentum, though execution risk remains pre-clinical.

17699416.summary: GABI is executing its capital return strategy following a May 2024 discontinuation vote. The fourth compulsory redemption of £45.0 million will occur at 68.63875 pence per share, representing the 30 June 2026 NAV less declared dividends. Approximately 65.56 million shares (38.14% redemption ratio) will be cancelled on 31 July 2026, with payments by 14 August. To date, four distributions totalling £266.6 million have been returned to shareholders, reducing share capital by 61.11%.
17699416.brief: GCP Asset Backed Income Fund announces fourth £45m compulsory redemption at 68.64p per share, 38.14% redemption ratio.
17699416.take: Positive for remaining shareholders as it reflects disciplined capital return during orderly wind-down; demonstrates management executing stated strategy with material progress on shareholder distributions.

17699417.summary: EnSilica announced €1.1 million in follow-on orders from a European satellite communications customer for its ENS92040 digital beamformer chip, comprising €350,000 for samples and €750,000 for product enhancements. The same customer previously ordered €2.5 million of the ASSP in August 2023. The ENS92040, developed with €5 million ESA funding, targets next-generation LEO, MEO and GEO satellite broadband terminals. High-volume production is anticipated from 2029 as new satellite constellations launch, with the satellite user terminal market expected to grow to over $3 billion by 2030.
17699417.brief: EnSilica secures €1.1m follow-on satellite chip orders from European customer for ENS92040 beamformer ASSP.
17699417.take: Repeat orders validate commercial traction for EnSilica's flagship satellite product and confirm market demand, though material revenue remains years away pending 2029 constellation deployments. Likely read as incrementally positive progression.

17699418.summary: NWF Group plc will release full year results for the year ended 31 May 2026 on Tuesday 28 July 2026. An analyst presentation video will be available at 8:00am hosted by CEO Chris Belsham and CFO Katie Shortland. A live investor presentation via Investor Meet Company will follow at 11:00am, open to existing and potential shareholders with pre-submission of questions available until 27 July at 9:00am.
17699418.brief: NWF Group to announce full year results for year ended 31 May 2026 on 28 July with analyst presentation and investor webinar.
17699418.take: Routine regulatory announcement of results release date and presentation arrangements; no material information disclosed; market will focus on actual results content when published.

17699375.summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 21 July 2026 via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $153.45 to $156.40, with a volume-weighted average of $155.3931. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, IHG has 148.6 million shares in issue excluding treasury stock. The purchase was authorised by shareholders at the May 2025 AGM and follows instructions issued in February 2026.
17699375.brief: IHG repurchased 1,000 shares at average price of $155.39 on 21 July for cancellation.
17699375.take: Routine share buyback execution under existing authority; modest scale suggests ongoing capital management rather than significant shareholder returns programme.

17699365.summary: SigmaRoc delivered H1 2026 revenue of £523.1m (+2.5% YoY) with underlying EBITDA of £131.2m (+11.3%), driving EBITDA margins up 200bps to 25.1%. Core volumes rose 1% in first increase in three years, with strong pricing offsetting modest revenue growth. EPS grew 12.2% to 5.23p. Covenant leverage improved to 1.66x from 2.04x. The group secured €825m investment-grade refinancing facility plus €300m accordion, and obtained permits for 64m tonnes additional limestone at Swedish Klinthagen operation. Board confident in delivering full-year consensus expectations.
17699365.brief: SigmaRoc posts strong H1 with 11.3% EBITDA growth and improved margins; maintains full-year guidance.
17699365.take: Solid operational delivery with margin expansion and deleveraging signals underlying business resilience; refinancing and permitting gains support growth strategy, likely viewed positively by market despite modest top-line growth.

17699367.summary: Fresnillo delivered Q2 attributable silver production of 10.9 moz, down 12.6% year-on-year due to planned Silverstream agreement end and lower grades at Saucito and Juanicipio. Gold production of 154.8 koz increased 13.8% sequentially but fell 1.9% year-on-year. First-half gold production of 290.9 koz decreased 7.3% year-on-year, primarily from lower ore grades and reduced processing at Herradura. Company maintains 2026 guidance: silver 42.0-46.5 moz, gold 500-550 koz. Interim results announced 4 August 2026.
17699367.brief: Fresnillo reports Q2 production with mixed results; gold up sequentially but silver down year-on-year; maintains full-year guidance.
17699367.take: Mixed operational performance with expected silver headwinds offset by gold resilience and maintained guidance signals management confidence, though year-on-year declines reflect challenging grade environment at key assets.

17699368.summary: RIT Capital Partners has set the tender price at £26.8515 per share for its £300 million share buyback programme announced in July 2026. This represents 85% of the preliminary diluted NAV per share as at 30 June 2026. The company will repurchase a maximum of 11,172,560 shares, with all participating shareholders having 8.21% of their tendered shares satisfied in full.
17699368.brief: RIT Capital Partners announces tender offer price of £26.8515 per share, equivalent to 85% of NAV.
17699368.take: Discount to NAV of 15% is standard for tender offers; buyback signals capital management but discount reflects execution costs and uncertainty, likely viewed as neutral by markets.

17699369.summary: On 21 July 2026, Experian purchased 317,396 of its own shares on the London Stock Exchange through BofA Securities Europe SA as part of its June 2026 share repurchase programme. Shares were bought at prices ranging from 2,668p to 2,700p, with a weighted average price of 2,693.81p. All purchased shares will be cancelled. Weekly transaction updates will be published on Experian's website.
17699369.brief: Experian repurchases 317,396 shares at 2,694p average price; shares to be cancelled.
17699369.take: Routine buyback execution reducing share count; signals management confidence in valuation but is non-event for investors absent broader programme context or strategic announcements.

17699370.summary: Dialight delivered Q1 sales growth significantly above its 3-5% guidance and achieved gross margins exceeding 45%, driving underlying profitability above the newly upgraded 15% return-on-sales target. The LED lighting company, which reported strong backlog in June 2026, has moved to a net cash position with expected further cash generation. Based on first quarter performance and current outlook, the board believes full-year profit for the year ending 31 March 2027 will exceed previous expectations.
17699370.brief: Dialight exceeds Q1 expectations with strong sales growth, gross margins above 45%, and upgraded profit forecast for FY2027.
17699370.take: Strong operational delivery with raised guidance suggests market confidence in demand and execution; net cash position and margin improvement signal operational leverage, likely supporting a positive market reception.

17699371.summary: George Ensor, portfolio manager for River UK Micro Cap Limited, has resigned from his position. He will continue managing the portfolio while the Board assesses options for a successor or alternative management structure. The Board is prioritising orderly transition and continuity of investment strategy, with further updates to follow.
17699371.brief: Portfolio manager George Ensor resigns from River UK Micro Cap Ltd; will remain in role during transition.
17699371.take: Key person risk materialises but managed exit is orderly; market likely views negatively absent clarity on replacement quality and timeline.

17699372.summary: Like-for-like sales rose 4.0% in 12 weeks to 19 July 2026, with year-to-date growth of 4.2%. The pub operator opened eight managed pubs and 15 franchised locations. Chairman Tim Martin cautioned that year profits will likely fall short of market expectations due to higher costs in food, labour, repairs, energy and business rates offsetting sales gains. Net debt is anticipated at £720 million, unchanged from year-end.
17699372.brief: Wetherspoon warns full-year profits likely below expectations amid cost pressures despite 4.0% like-for-like sales growth.
17699372.take: Negative sentiment likely: strong sales growth negated by profit warning from cost inflation, signalling margin squeeze in hospitality sector and challenging operating environment ahead.

17699373.summary: Melia Hotels International terminated all hotel management and commercialization services in Cuba effective 24 July 2026, directly affecting Ceiba's 5 hotels operated through Cuban joint ventures Miramar and TosCuba. The decision follows US Executive Order 14404 (1 May 2026) which expanded sanctions targeting foreign entities supporting Cuba's government, specifically designating GAESA and the Ministry of Tourism. Ceiba states occupancy has already deteriorated severely from reduced airlift, so additional 2026 income impact is expected to be minimal. Management is analyzing options including potential operational suspension, capital expenditure projects, and strategic search for alternative operators.
17699373.brief: Melia Hotels terminates Cuba operations effective 24 July 2026, impacting Ceiba's 5 hotels following US sanctions expansion.
17699373.take: Negative read: material disruption to Ceiba's core Cuba hotel operations with uncertain recovery timeline and operational/financial risks, though management downplays near-term earnings impact given already-weak occupancy.

17699374.summary: Consumer Legal Services division grew revenues 2% to £11.3m with profits up 52% to £1.2m, driven by higher National Accident Law settlements (£5.7m cash). Critical Care revenues rose 3% to £8.5m with strong expert witness services (+11%), though case management services declined 8%. Free cash flow surged 42% to £2.1m, reducing net debt from £3.2m to £1.0m. Board expects H2 2026 settlement volumes to decline materially due to lower prior enquiry placements, moderately increasing net debt.
17699374.brief: NAHL reports H1 2026 revenues of £19.7m (+3%), underlying operating profit up 8% to £3.4m, net debt cut 68% to £1.0m.
17699374.take: Solid H1 performance with strong cash generation and debt reduction offsets guidance for weaker H2 due to anticipated lower settlements; board exploring strategic options to accelerate shareholder value.

17699378.summary: Q3 pro forma revenue grew 3.2% to £1,024m with volumes up 2.3%, outpacing the grocery market. Manufactured volume growth accelerated to 0.7% versus market decline of -0.4%, driven by legacy Greencore (+0.3%) and Bakkavor (+1.0%). The company raised FY26 Adjusted Operating Profit guidance for continuing operations (UK) to £234-242m, representing 17-21% growth versus prior year's £201m, above current analyst consensus of £224m (range £219-231m). Bakkavor integration progressing well with ~£15m in-year cost synergies expected and c.£80m annual target maintained; US business continues sale exploration.
17699378.brief: Greencore upgrades FY26 profit guidance to £234-242m, ahead of £224m consensus, on strong Q3 trading and Bakkavor synergies.
17699378.take: Positive surprise: guidance upgrade of £10-18m above consensus, underpinned by volume growth momentum, margin expansion and accelerating synergy delivery from Bakkavor deal. Market likely receptive to earnings beat visibility.

17699388.summary: Orosur Mining completed a 24-hole, 5,533m drilling campaign at its El Pantano project in Santa Cruz province, Argentina, identifying a major epithermal gold/silver system. The company fulfilled Phase 2 commitments of US$2m ahead of schedule, exercising its option to retain 100% ownership from joint venture partner Deseado Dorado. Drilling revealed structurally controlled gold-bearing vein corridors in the west (intersections including 27.12m at 0.20g/t Au) and pathfinder-rich silica cap zones in the east, supporting the epithermal mineral system hypothesis. The NI 43-101 Technical Report was completed and filed July 2026.
17699388.brief: Orosur completes drilling at El Pantano gold/silver project in Argentina; achieves 100% ownership after meeting JV obligations.
17699388.take: Positive sentiment driven by successful maiden drilling confirming exploration thesis and securing full project ownership; however, grades are modest and early-stage status means years to potential production, appealing mainly to exploration-focused investors.

17699387.summary: Reach reported H1 2026 revenue of £232.9m (down 9.0% YoY), with adjusted operating profit of £43.0m and 18.5% margin. Print revenue fell 8.3% to £178m due to 22% circulation volume decline; digital revenue dropped 11.4% to £54.2m from 40% on-platform page view decline. Adjusted operating costs fell 10.3%, beating 5-6% target. Net debt increased to £47.5m (0.4x leverage). Interim dividend rebased to 1.44p from 2.88p to preserve cash for growth investment and pension obligations (£57m due 2026, stepping to £15m from 2028).
17699387.brief: Reach PLC on track for FY26 guidance despite 9% revenue decline from print/digital headwinds and Google referral traffic losses.
17699387.take: Revenue decline and dividend cut signal structural industry challenges, offset by margin resilience, cost discipline, and strategic traction (40k subscriptions, 6% direct revenue growth ex-local). Cautious outlook and pension bridging costs until 2028 may temper investor enthusiasm despite on-track guidance.

17699386.summary: Harena Rare Earths has signed a Project Development Funding Agreement with the US International Development Finance Corporation (DFC), committing US$4.84 million towards development of the Ampasindava ionic clay rare earths project in Madagascar. Funds will support permitting, environmental and social studies, and a proof-of-concept pilot plant, advancing towards a Definitive Feasibility Study. DFC has also retained rights to consider substantially larger financing as the project progresses towards construction, signalling long-term US government strategic interest in securing Western-aligned critical mineral supply chains.
17699386.brief: Harena Rare Earths secures US$4.84m development funding from US International Development Finance Corporation for Madagascar rare earths project.
17699386.take: Positive signal: US government backing validates project economics and addresses funding risk; DFC's willingness to consider larger future investment suggests confidence in development pathway and strategic importance.

17699383.summary: Personal Group Holdings (AIM: PGH), a workforce benefits and insurance provider, delivered H1 2026 revenue of £25.7m (up 10% YoY) with >90% recurring revenue, and adjusted EBITDA of £6.7m (up 22% YoY). Insurance revenue grew 11% to £19.4m driven by record H1 sales of £8.1m annualised premium income and 12% growth in annual premium income to £42.4m. Benefits & Rewards revenue increased 9% to £5.7m. The company maintains a strong balance sheet with £29.4m cash and no debt, with board confidence that FY 2026 trading remains aligned with market expectations of £54.4m revenue and £14.1m adjusted EBITDA.
17699383.brief: Personal Group reports 10% revenue growth to £25.7m and 22% adjusted EBITDA growth to £6.7m in H1 2026, trading in line with expectations.
17699383.take: Solid operational delivery with double-digit growth in adjusted EBITDA outpacing revenue growth, supported by strong cash generation and no debt; guidance reiteration and record insurance sales provide market reassurance, likely read as positive.

17699382.summary: Mulberry Group reported FY26 revenue of £125.5m (up 4%, accelerating to 11% in H2) with gross margin improving to 71.9% from 66.8% through full-price discipline. Reported loss before tax narrowed to £8.9m from £32.2m; underlying loss fell to £8.0m from £24.1m. Positive underlying EBITDA of £0.8m (vs. loss of £16.8m prior year) marks a turning point. CEO Andrea Baldo highlighted strong UK customer reconnection (over 50% returning customers) and double-digit LFL growth across regions in H2, with FY27 Q1 showing continued momentum at 23% group revenue growth.
17699382.brief: Mulberry returns to growth with revenue up 4% to £125.5m; swings to positive underlying EBITDA of £0.8m as losses narrow.
17699382.take: Market likely reads this positively as credible early-stage turnaround: revenue inflection, profitability milestones hit, and brand repositioning gaining traction despite challenging luxury sector; risks remain on execution and macro environment.

17699376.summary: On 21 July 2026, Plus500 purchased 9,475 ordinary shares through Panmure Liberum at prices ranging from 4,036p to 4,108p, with a volume-weighted average price of 4,079.03p. Following this transaction, 68,951,512 shares remain in issue (excluding 45,936,865 treasury shares). The repurchased shares will be held in treasury. This continues the buyback programme announced 9 February 2026 and commenced 16 February 2026.
17699376.brief: Plus500 repurchased 9,475 shares at 4,036-4,108p as part of its buyback programme.
17699376.take: Routine capital management activity with immaterial share count reduction; no material implications for investors but demonstrates continued capital return discipline.

17699377.summary: Shuka conditionally raised £750,000 through subscription of 18.75m shares at 4p per share with Menel Energy and Resources Limited, representing a 53.9% premium to the 21 July closing price of 2.6p. Menel will hold ~12.3% post-admission and appoint a non-executive director. Simultaneously, Shuka amended its shareholder loan with GMI, extending repayment to 31 December 2027, reducing principal by US$300k following a US$250k payment due by 31 July, and issuing 2.98m shares as a 7.5% restructuring fee. First tranche of £375k already received; second tranche expected by 31 August 2026.
17699377.brief: Shuka Minerals raises £750k from Zambian investor Menel at 54% premium; restructures £1.59m GMI loan to December 2027.
17699377.take: Strategic backing from experienced African investor provides near-term liquidity and validates management's capital-raising ability, though significant dilution and extended debt repayment may concern existing shareholders seeking near-term value realisation.

17699389.summary: Henry Boot (land, property and housebuilding) reports H1 2026 performance materially below H1 2025 due to elevated macroeconomic uncertainty. Hallam Land sold 556 plots (vs 1,222 prior year); full-year 2026 plot sales expected materially below 3,957 (FY25). Stonebridge Homes completed 72 homes (85 prior year), facing 5% build cost inflation and higher mortgage impacts; expected 2026 operating loss. HBD's industrial portfolio saw modest valuation declines. Board now expects FY26 profit before tax significantly below £20.4m market consensus. Net debt rose to £132.9m (£108.0m end-2025); lending covenants amended June 2026.
17699389.brief: Henry Boot issues profit warning; H1 2026 trading below prior year amid market headwinds and cost pressures.
17699389.take: Profit warning with significantly downward guidance signals deteriorating market conditions and execution headwinds across divisions, likely to trigger negative sentiment despite medium-term portfolio strength.

17699379.summary: Energean PLC successfully commissioned its second oil train on the Energean Power FPSO on 13 July 2026, increasing total liquids processing capacity from 18 kbbl/d to 31 kbbl/d. Production was tested at rates up to 21 kbbl/d, with further testing expected in August 2026. H1 2026 Israeli liquids production averaged 10 kbbl/d (13 kbbl/d excluding a 41-day geopolitical shutdown); H2 2026 guidance projects 17-21 kbbl/d average, strengthening the company's position as Israel's largest liquids producer with higher Brent-linked revenue exposure.
17699379.brief: Energean completes second oil train on FPSO, boosting liquids capacity to 31 kbbl/d and increasing Brent-linked revenues.
17699379.take: Positive operationally; capacity expansion and higher commodity-linked revenues should support earnings growth, though execution risk remains on further testing and geopolitical sensitivities persist.

17699380.summary: H1 2026 attributable production reached 111,429 oz gold and 3.1M oz silver (151,830 gold equivalent ounces).
